unitMappingSet.title: Mapping from "UnitOfMeasure/CatalogSymbol" values in the "POSC Unit Dictionary V2.2" dictionary to "unit/symbol" values in the "Energistics Unit of Measure Dictionary V1.0" dictionary.
unitMappingSet.description: This document asserts a mapping from one dictionary to another. The mapping is between items which appear to have the same semantics (i.e., are aliases). All items in the "from" dictionary are listed. For each "from" item, if no "to" item is listed then there does not appear to be a direct mapping. This mapping is provided as an aid to migration and Energistics makes no guarantee of its accuracy. The POSC Unit of Measure Dictionary V2.2 was originally derived from the POSC Epicentre Data Model whose unit of measure data was based on the 1984 "The SPE Metric System of Units and SPE METRIC STANDARD". This data is distributed under the Energistics License Agreement at http://www.energistics.org. Use of this file constitutes agreement with the Energistics License Agreement. Copyright (c) 2014 Energistics. All rights reserved.

unitMap.note: The parachor class was eliminated because the units depend on the formula being used.
The parachor class was eliminated because the units depend on the formula being used.
Contains year as a component. See mapping discussion for year.
Contains year as a component. See mapping discussion for year.
The old POSC deprecated definition was bits per second.
Assume that the old conversion is correct and convert to a supported unit with the same base.
Contains year as a component. See mapping discussion for year.
Contains year as a component. See mapping discussion for year.
Assume that the old conversion is correct and convert to a supported unit with the same base.
Utilizes a volume at standard conditions. See mapping discussion about conditions of measurement.
Contains year as a component. See mapping discussion for year.
Utilizes a volume at standard conditions. See mapping discussion about conditions of measurement.
Utilizes a volume at standard conditions. See mapping discussion about conditions of measurement.

Utilizes a volume at standard conditions. See mapping discussion about conditions of measurement.
Utilizes a volume at standard conditions. See mapping discussion about conditions of measurement.
Utilizes a volume at standard conditions. See mapping discussion about conditions of measurement.
The parachor class was eliminated because the units depend on the formula being used.

See mapping discussion about conditions of measurement. The old conversion factors converted from gauge to absolute assuming 1 atmosphere of ambient pressure (i.e., it added one atmosphere of pressure).
The concept of "nonDarcy flow coefficient" could not be validated.
Contains year as a component. See mapping discussion for year.
Contains year as a component. See mapping discussion for year.
Utilizes a volume at standard conditions. See mapping discussion about conditions of measurement.
Assume that the old conversion is correct and convert to a supported unit with the same base.
Utilizes a volume at standard conditions. See mapping discussion about conditions of measurement.
The concept of "specific productivity index" could not be validated.
Utilizes a volume at standard conditions. See mapping discussion about conditions of measurement.
The underlying definition is for an angular velocity but the general concept of cycle per second could also be interpreted as a frequency which would map to Hertz.
Contains year as a component. See mapping discussion for year.
The concept of an equivalent could not be validated.
The concept of an equivalent could not be validated.
The concept of an equivalent could not be validated.
The concept of an equivalent could not be validated.
Contains year as a component. See mapping discussion for year.
Utilizes a volume at standard conditions. See mapping discussion about conditions of measurement.
Utilizes a volume at standard conditions. See mapping discussion about conditions of measurement.
Assume that the old conversion is correct and convert to a supported unit with the same base.
Utilizes a volume at standard conditions. See mapping discussion about conditions of measurement.
Assume that the old conversion is correct and convert to a supported unit with the same base.
Choose the most appropriate definition of either 1/50th per cent or 1/100th percent and convert to a supported "volume per volume" unit.
Contains year as a component. See mapping discussion for year.
Utilizes a volume at standard conditions. See mapping discussion about conditions of measurement.
Utilizes a volume at standard conditions. See mapping discussion about conditions of measurement.
Utilizes a volume at standard conditions. See mapping discussion about conditions of measurement.
Contains year as a component. See mapping discussion for year.
Utilizes a volume at standard conditions. See mapping discussion about conditions of measurement. Assume that the old conversion is correct and convert to a supported unit with the same base.
Utilizes a volume at standard conditions. See mapping discussion about conditions of measurement. Assume that the old conversion is correct and convert to a supported unit with the same base.

Unitlesss concepts are not supported.
